sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
MOUSE didn't change the board (board_changed: false). Let me think about this differently.
Looking at the current state:
- Character is at the right edge (cols 60-62)
- Yellow L-shape is at cols 51-59
- Charcoal L-shape is at cols 0-8
I've been moving around a lot without completing the level. Let me reconsider what the goal might be.
Looking at the history, I started with the character on the right side, moved LEFT many times to go to the left panel, then moved RIGHT back to the right panel. The yellow L-shape has been partially consumed (from 45px to 29px), and the yellow border bar has shrunk (from some initial value to 9px).
Wait, I think the key insight is that the yellow border bar on the right edge is shrinking as I move. This might be a "remaining steps" or "fuel" bar. The goal might be to consume the yellow L-shape before the bar runs out.
But I've already consumed some of the yellow L-shape (from 45px to 29px). Let me continue trying to consume it by moving through it.
Actually, looking at the current image, the character is at the right edge, and the yellow L-shape is to the left of the character. The character seems to be facing left (the white dots are on the left side). Let me try moving LEFT to go through the yellow L-shape again.
But wait, I already tried this and the character moved past the L-shape without fully consuming it. Maybe I need to position the character differently.
Let me try a different approach: maybe the goal is to consume the charcoal L-shape, not the yellow one. The charcoal L-shape is still at full size (45px). Let me move LEFT to go back to the left panel and try to consume the charcoal L-shape.
Actually, I've been spending too many actions. Let me try to complete the level by moving LEFT through the yellow L-shape and then continuing to the charcoal L-shape.
